data.title: Microservices Adoption
data.description: YouTube uses a microservices architecture to handle different functionalities such as video processing, search, and recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Distributed system with RESTful APIs and gRPC communication.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: CDNs are used to distribute video content efficiently across the globe, reducing load times and buffering.
data.technical_spec: Multiple CDNs including Google's proprietary infrastructure, supporting HTTP/2 and QUIC protocols.

data.title: Scalability
data.description: The architecture supports large-scale user interaction and video uploads through autoscaling technologies.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes containerization technologies and Kubernetes for orchestration.

data.title: Data Storage Systems
data.description: Handles large volumes of data through distributed databases and object storage for video files.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Ensures high availability and balanced load across servers and services.
data.technical_spec: Global Load Balancer, with Anycast capabilities.
